questions.question: What is Bholi’s real name?
questions.answer: Bholi’s real name is Sulekha.

questions.question: Why was Sulekha called Bholi?
questions.answer: She was called Bholi because she was considered simple and dull.

questions.question: Who was Bholi’s father?
questions.answer: Bholi’s father was Ramlal.

questions.question: What problem did Bholi have in speaking?
questions.answer: She had a stammering problem.

questions.question: Which illness disfigured Bholi’s face?
questions.answer: Smallpox disfigured Bholi’s face.

questions.question: How many daughters did Ramlal have?
questions.answer: Ramlal had four daughters.

questions.question: At what age was Bholi sent to school?
questions.answer: Bholi was sent to school at the age of seven.

questions.question: Who encouraged Bholi at school?
questions.answer: Her teacher encouraged her.

questions.question: How did the teacher treat Bholi?
questions.answer: The teacher treated Bholi kindly and patiently.

questions.question: What gave Bholi confidence?
questions.answer: Education gave Bholi confidence.

questions.question: Who was Bishamber Nath?
questions.answer: Bishamber Nath was a wealthy grocer.

questions.question: What did Bishamber Nath demand?
questions.answer: He demanded dowry.

questions.question: Why did Bholi initially agree to marriage?
questions.answer: She agreed to protect her parents’ honour.

questions.question: What did Bholi do at the wedding?
questions.answer: She refused to marry Bishamber Nath.

questions.question: What quality did Bholi show at the end?
questions.answer: She showed confidence and self-respect.

questions.question: What social evil is shown in the story?
questions.answer: The dowry system is shown as a social evil.

questions.question: Who represents progressive thinking in the story?
questions.answer: The teacher represents progressive thinking.

questions.question: What promise does Bholi make at the end?
questions.answer: She promises to serve her parents.

questions.question: Where does Bholi decide to work?
questions.answer: She decides to teach in the same school.

questions.question: What was Bholi’s condition at birth?
questions.answer: She was fair and pretty at birth.

questions.question: Why was Bholi afraid to speak earlier?
questions.answer: She was afraid due to ridicule and neglect.

questions.question: What does education give Bholi?
questions.answer: Education gives her courage and dignity.

questions.question: Who is the author of ‘Bholi’?
questions.answer: The author of ‘Bholi’ is K.A. Abbas.

questions.question: What does Bholi’s refusal symbolise?
questions.answer: It symbolises empowerment and self-respect.

questions.question: What is the central theme of ‘Bholi’?
questions.answer: Education and empowerment of women.
